title: Guru Granth Sahib
subtitle: The Living Guru of the Sikhs
description: The central religious scripture of Sikhism, a collection of hymns (shabads) and writings by the Sikh Gurus and various saints from different religious backgrounds, compiled by Guru Arjan in 1604.
summary: The Guru Granth Sahib is regarded as the living Guru of the Sikhs, containing 1,430 pages of hymns (shabads) and poetry. It includes writings of Sikh Gurus as well as Hindu and Muslim saints (Bhagats), emphasizing the oneness of God (Ik Onkar), equality of all humans, service, honest living, and meditation on God's name. It is written in Gurmukhi script and comprises multiple languages including Punjabi, Sanskrit, Persian, and Braj Bhasha.

chapters.title: Japji Sahib – The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: There is One God, the Eternal Truth. He is the Creator, without fear and without hate. He is timeless and without form. He is beyond birth and death, self-existent. By the Guru's grace, He is realized.
By meditating on the True Name, I live. O my mother, in this way I am sustained. The True Name is my support and my treasure; it removes all my sorrows and fulfills all my needs.
If I please Him, I am blessed. The True Guru has united me with Him. The word of the Guru is the inner music; hearing it, I find peace. I am a sacrifice to my Guru.

chapters.title: Sri Rag – The Feeling of Beauty (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: Truth is higher than everything, but higher still is true living. Without the True Guru, no one finds the way; through the Guru's word, God is known and realized.

There are six schools of philosophy, six teachers, and six sets of doctrines. But the Teacher of teachers is the One Lord, who appears in many forms. O Lord, preserve this world in peace through the Guru's grace.

chapters.title: Asa Di Var – The Song of Hope (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: He who created the world and gives sustenance to all—He alone is the True Lord. He is the Giver, the Doer, the Cause of causes. Nanak says: ever True is the Creator, forever True is His Name.

chapters.title: Sukhmani Sahib – The Psalm of Peace (Guru Arjan)

verses.text: Meditating on the Lord of the Universe, I find peace. By the Guru's grace, all sorrows depart. The Lord's name is the treasure that fills the heart; it is the jewel of peace, the essence of all wisdom.
He who looks upon all with a single eye, and knows all as part of the One—that person is truly wise. Nanak says: only such a one knows the true way.

chapters.title: Bhairo Rag – The Song of Detachment (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: Why do you go to the forest in search of the Divine? He dwells in all, yet remains distinct. As the fragrance dwells in the flower, as the reflection in the mirror—so does God dwell within you. Seek Him in your own heart.

chapters.title: Malar Rag – The Song of Devotion

verses.text: The Lord is the river, and we are the fish in it. How can the fish know the river? Wherever the fish goes, it is still in the water. So too, wherever we are, we are in the Lord.
Farid says: if you desire to be loved by the Lord, then do not sleep at night, nor eat to fill your belly. See the Beloved in all others; and if you see not, then know that you have lost the way.

chapters.title: Ramkali Rag – The Song of Mystical Wisdom

verses.text: One who recognizes the True Guru is blessed. The world is deluded by doubt, but the Guru banishes all fear. Realizing the Word, one obtains honor. Nanak says: without the True Guru, the world is lost.

chapters.title: Anand Sahib – The Song of Bliss (Guru Amar Das)

verses.text: Blissful is the state of the one who sings the Lord's praises. I am in ecstasy, listening to the divine melody. The Lord's name is ambrosial; drinking it, all thirst is quenched. Nanak says: sing the Lord's praises, and merge in the divine light.
